channel.title: Posts on Alan Aw
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Alan Aw

item.title: Computing Euclidean Distances
item.description: In recent work involving resampling, I had to implement a fast procedure involving computing pairwise distances from a large matrix many times. This led me to the following investigation. Given an array \(\mathbf{X}\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times p}\), where the rows index \(n\) samples and columns index \(p\) features, how fast does it take to compute pairwise Euclidean distances for each pair of samples? To answer this question, we can performing a timing analysis with the help of the microbenchmark package.

item.title: Review of “Who We Are and How We Got Here"
item.description: One of my goals this year is to read more books and to think critically about them. I’ve started a spreadsheet tracking books I’ve read and books I’m reading, and I’m excited to share it here. I just finished reading David Reich’s book on ancient DNA (aDNA) and human history, Reich, David. Who We Are and How We Got Here: Ancient DNA and the new science of the human past.

item.title: Lagrange Multipliers Revisited
item.description: Happy New Year! This is my first post, and I intend to use this space to write about my thoughts and learning. Any constructive feedback would be appreciated! Now for the actual post. I’ve been thinking about mathematics over Winter break, mostly because I often find myself overlooking details about important results and consequently learning maths not as thoroughly as I’d like. I find this especially true with regard to analysis (or continuous maths in general), so I try to revisit the maths every now and then.
